description: Run the first proxy comparison for the `jumprelu` family at K=128 using the prototype already added under `sparsify/`. Keep the round focused on establishing whether this family can materially improve the anchor-quality regime before any smaller-K or throughput tradeoffs.
self_review: This is the narrowest coherent follow-up because there is still no empirical signal for the new family. Risk remains runtime overhead from the dense encode path, but that should be interpreted as implementation cost unless quality is also clearly unpromising.
